spec: docs/jcp-update-spec.md (covers framework) + docs/jcp-notes-compilation/RRL Website Screenshots/RRL Website Modifications.pdf (note for this delta)
audit_scope: delta · RRL note changes only (not full page)

findings.issue: Canonical channel count drifted from 78 → 79 after this commit (−Mard + Molton Brown + Facegym). The mega-menu suffix still shows '78 channels' on every page (~25 files). The /jcp/ hero stat tile still says '78 channels · 68 live · 5 in-progress · 5 planned'. The /jcp/channels/ section label still says '78 channels'. Home page /index.html mentions '78 channels' in 4 places. Apex reader will see two different numbers depending on which page they land on.
findings.fix: Update tools/site_chrome.py PLATFORMS suffix '78 channels' → '79 channels' and rerun tools/inject_chrome.py to propagate. Manually update jcp/index.html hero stat tile + intro paragraph + Ecosystem section blurb (3 places) and home /index.html (4 places). Recompute the live/in-progress/planned breakdown — new totals are 68 Live / 5 In-Progress / 6 Planned (Mard −1 live, LensCrafters +1 live, Coach +1 live, La Martina −1 live +1 in-progress, Facegym +1 in-progress, Molton Brown +1 planned).

findings.issue: Brand name rendered as 'Coverstory' (lowercase 's') on /jcp/channels/. Source RRL note + brand identity uses 'CoverStory' (camelCase). PDF directory tools/build_jcp_channels_md.py also uses 'Coverstory'. Brand site is coverstory.co.in (lowercase, but site domains are case-insensitive).
findings.fix: Update PDF_CHANNELS row 58 in tools/build_jcp_channels_md.py from 'Coverstory' → 'CoverStory'. Rebuild — channel card label updates automatically.

findings.issue: Brand name rendered as 'WestElm' (PascalCase) on /jcp/channels/. RBL synthetic data has 'West Elm' (with space). Wikipedia + brand identity use 'West Elm' (with space). RBL data set canonically has the space.
findings.fix: Update tools/scratch/rbl_synthetic.json westelm.brand from 'West Elm' to be reflected on the page — but the page renders from PDF_CHANNELS which has 'WestElm'. Decide one canonical: 'West Elm' (matches brand) is recommended. Update PDF_CHANNELS row 47 'WestElm' → 'West Elm'.

findings.issue: RRL note says Coach and LensCrafters are 'live'. Site flips both from 'In-Progress' → 'Live' in PDF_CHANNELS — correct per note. BUT the Channel Coverage PDF (Feb 2026, the canonical source) lists them as 'In-Progress'. The RRL note (newer, dated 2026-04-30 by inference) overrides. No issue with the change itself, but no source citation on the page explains why these went Live since the Feb 2026 PDF.
findings.fix: Either accept the change silently (the note IS the latest source) OR add a tiny 'updated 2026-04-30 per RRL note' line on the channel cards for these two. Recommend silent — apex doesn't need every change-log; the PDF directory verbatim says 21-Feb-2026 already.

findings.issue: Card alt text and title attribute show '7Eleven' (no hyphen) but rest of site (subnav chip, /jcp/7eleven/ page title) uses '7-Eleven'. Source PDF uses '7Eleven'. Pick one.
findings.fix: Change tools/scratch/non_rbl_channels.json 7eleven.name from '7-Eleven' (already done) to ensure card label renders '7-Eleven'. Also update the lightbox caption helper in tools/build_jcp_channels_page.py if it hardcodes the brand spelling.

findings.issue: The 3 web-wrapped app cards (Superdry · Hamleys · Sephora) link their ↗ icon to play.google.com/store/search?q=... rather than a specific app. Apex reader who clicks discovers the search-results page, not the canonical listing. Source RRL note ships only the screenshot; doesn't name the package ID, so the search fallback is the only viable link. Acceptable but worth noting.
findings.fix: Manually find each app's package id (`com.superdry.india`-style) once and pin in non_rbl_play.json/EXTRA_APPS. Or accept the search-link as good enough.

strengths: Source traceability is perfect on this delta: every one of the 10 RRL note items implemented exactly as specified (Mard removal, 3 URL fixes, 4 channel additions, 3 web-wrapped apps, WestElm category fix). No drift from the note.

Asset pipeline is clean: 10 new images copied, normalised, pushed to GCS, all serving 200 from CDN. Per-slug folder convention preserved. The 3 web-wrapped apps got their own slug-folders (superdry-app/, hamleys-app/, sephora-app/) so they don't collide with the storefront cards of the same brand.

The web-wrapped app card pattern (separate cards with 'web-wrapped app' / 'consumer app' badges and no Play Store icon overlay) is a clean visual treatment that distinguishes them from native consumer apps without misleading.
Honesty register is tight: status pill changes (Coach Live, LensCrafters Live, La Martina In-Progress, Molton Brown Planned, Facegym In-Progress) all map exactly to what the note says. No overclaims.
Visual completeness: the gallery now shows 34 storefronts + 18 apps + 30 skipped = 82 cards. All images load from CDN; layout flows; filter chips still work.
